desc: Dali anchors northwest Yunnan with Bai heritage towns, Erhai Lake, and Cangshan foothills, fitting travelers who want scenic culture and a slower pace outside China's megacities while planning around summer monsoon periods.
hls: Erhai Lake loop landscapes with village-to-village cycling
Bai architecture and courtyards in Dali Old Town and Xizhou
Cangshan mountain access for cableway viewpoints and short trails
Temperate highland climate ideal for spring/autumn pacing

todo.name: Dali Old Town walls and Foreigner Street lanes
todo.desc: Walk the gate towers, temple alleys, and morning produce streets before crowds build.

todo.name: Three Pagodas (Chongsheng Temple)
todo.desc: Visit early for clear Cangshan-backdrop photos and quieter temple grounds.

todo.name: Erhai west-shore cycle: Caicun to Xizhou
todo.desc: Ride between lakeside wetlands, village cafés, and classic Bai courtyard architecture.

todo.name: Xizhou market and Bai courtyard houses
todo.desc: Time your visit for the morning market and local dairy snack stalls.

todo.name: Cangshan cableway + mid-mountain boardwalk
todo.desc: Use the cableway for alpine panoramas and a short ridge walk if weather holds.

todo.name: Shaxi Ancient Town day trip
todo.desc: Add a deeper Tea Horse Road stop if you have a full extra day from Dali.

wifi.note: Decent in modern hotels, variable in traditional courtyard inns.

prac.fltNote: From the UK/Ireland, typical chains are long-haul to Kunming/Chengdu/Guangzhou/Shanghai (sometimes Hong Kong), then a domestic leg to Dali (DLU) or high-speed rail via Kunming; Lijiang (LJG) is a practical alternate airport for multi-stop Yunnan itineraries.
prac.lang: Mandarin Chinese is the primary language; English support is limited outside larger hotels and transport nodes.
prac.tz: China Standard Time (UTC+8) year-round; no daylight saving time.
prac.visa: China entry is nationality-specific; many travelers still require a visa in advance, though some cities/routes may allow limited transit-without-visa schemes.
alerts: June-September monsoon downpours can trigger short-notice Cangshan cableway suspensions and slower mountain-road transfers.
National holiday surges (Lunar New Year, May Day, and early-October Golden Week) raise lodging prices and reduce same-day transport flexibility.
Many old-town lanes are pedestrianized stone streets; confirm hotel pickup points if arriving with heavy luggage.

sweet: Mar-May and Oct-Nov for the best comfort-to-value balance; skies are clearer than peak monsoon, while avoiding the highest domestic-holiday crowd pressure.
